paper.title_en: <math><mi>β</mi></math>-spectrum shapes of first-forbidden transitions with <math><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><msup><mi>I</mi><mi>π</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mo>−</mo></msup></mrow></math> in <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Rb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>92</mn></mmultiscripts></math> and <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cs</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>142</mn></mmultiscripts></math> decays
paper.abstract_en: The electron spectra of the <math><mi>β</mi></math> decays of <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Rb</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>92</mn></mmultiscripts></math> and <math><mmultiscripts><mi>Cs</mi><mprescripts/><none/><mn>142</mn></mmultiscripts></math>, key contributors to the reactor antineutrino spectrum, were measured at the IGISOL facility using radioactive beams of high isotopic purity. The shapes of the measured <math><mi>β</mi></math> spectra were compared with various <math><mi>β</mi></math>-shape models, including first-forbidden correction factors for <math><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">Δ</mi><msup><mi>I</mi><mi>π</mi></msup><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mo>−</mo></msup></mrow></math> ground-state to ground-state transitions. Comparisons with previous experimental results are also provided. The shapes of the newly measured <math><mi>β</mi></math> spectra are well reproduced employing feedings extracted from total absorption gamma spectroscopy measurements.
